---
title: "Setting yaboot to boot OSX by default"
date: 2005-09-09
forum: Apple PPC Users
---

### Post by Shuriken on 2005-09-09
Hello,

I recently installed Ubuntu on my PowerBook G4. I also have OSX on the machine. I'd like to have OSX as the "primary" system, so that yaboot would boot it automatically after a few seconds if nothing is pressed. Currently, Ubuntu is booted by default - I tried to modify my yaboot.conf after reading `man yaboot.conf`, but I can't get it to work.

My current yaboot.conf looks like this:

```

## yaboot.conf generated by the Ubuntu installer
##
## run: "man yaboot.conf" for details. Do not make changes until you have!!
## see also: /usr/share/doc/yaboot/examples for example configurations.
##
## For a dual-boot menu, add one or more of:
## bsd=/dev/hdaX, macos=/dev/hdaY, macosx=/dev/hdaZ

macosx=/dev/hda3
boot=/dev/hda5
device=/pci@f4000000/ata-6@d/disk@0:
partition=2
root=/dev/hda2
delay=5
timeout=30
install=/usr/lib/yaboot/yaboot
magicboot=/usr/lib/yaboot/ofboot
enablecdboot
defaultos=macosx

image=/boot/vmlinux
        label=Linux
        read-only
        initrd=/boot/initrd.img
        append="quiet splash"

image=/boot/vmlinux.old
        label=old
        read-only
        initrd=/boot/initrd.img.old
        append="quiet splash"


```

So, if anyone could help me with getting OSX to boot by default, I'd be grateful.

Thanks in advance,

Shuriken

---

### Post by king_arthur on 2005-09-09
I am not 100% sure as I don't have a power pc install handy however, if I remember correctly, yaboot, pretty much as Lilo needs to be run as a binary after you change the configuration file.

Type **ybin** after modifying you yaboot.conf file and see what happens.
That should modify your bootloader accordingly.

Good luck!

/P

---

### Post by stuporglue on 2005-09-24
ybin is correct.

If you run "ybin -v" you can watch for errors, and get a funny message about holy penguin pee.

---
